nodes.label: Regression Modeling
nodes.description: A statistical method used in MMM to derive an equation describing the KPI by assigning coefficients to independent variables.

nodes.label: Ridge Regression
nodes.description: A regularized regression method used in Robyn to handle multicollinearity among media variables.

nodes.label: Adstock
nodes.description: A concept representing the carryover effect of marketing spend over time, where effects persist beyond the investment week.

nodes.label: Saturation
nodes.description: A concept representing the diminishing returns of marketing spend on a target metric as investment increases.

nodes.label: Prophet
nodes.description: A time-series forecast package by Meta used for trend and seasonality decomposition in Robyn.

nodes.label: Meta
nodes.description: The organization that collaborated with Deloitte to develop the Measurement 360 framework for advertisers.

nodes.label: Robyn
nodes.description: An R package for Marketing Mix Modeling that automates model specification, optimization, and calibration.

nodes.label: Nevergrad
nodes.description: Meta's gradient-free optimization platform used by Robyn for multi-objective hyperparameter optimization.

nodes.label: Budget Allocator
nodes.description: A feature within Robyn designed to optimize marketing spend across different media channels.

nodes.label: Data Collection
nodes.description: One of the most important steps in an MMM study involving collecting time-series data for sales, media, non-media marketing and macroeconomic factors.

nodes.label: Feature Engineering
nodes.description: The process of transforming raw data into features that better represent the underlying problem to predictive models, critical for MMM accuracy.

nodes.label: Model Selection
nodes.description: The process of choosing the final model for analysis, often involving trade-offs between statistical quality and business plausibility.

nodes.label: ROI
nodes.description: Return on Investment, a crucial metric for measuring the effectiveness of marketing channel investments.

nodes.label: KPI
nodes.description: Key Performance Indicator, the primary metric or dependent variable that the MMM will measure against such as sales or website visits.

nodes.label: Time Series Analysis
nodes.description: Used to determine seasonality trends which can be incorporated in the final model using tools like Prophet.

nodes.label: Budget Allocation
nodes.description: A feature in Robyn that calculates optimum cross-media budget splits based on response or target efficiency.

nodes.label: Carryover Effect
nodes.description: The lagged effect of advertising on consumer purchase behavior, where ads seen earlier influence purchases later.

nodes.label: Shape Effect
nodes.description: The non-linear relationship between media spend and sales, accounting for ad saturation and diminishing returns at high levels of spend.

nodes.label: Adstock Function
nodes.description: A transformation function used to model the carryover effect by weighting current and past media spend over a duration.

nodes.label: Hill Function
nodes.description: A nonlinear function implemented in Robyn for flexible transformation into S- or L-shape saturation curves.

nodes.label: Bayesian Methods
nodes.description: State-of-the-art flavors of MMMs that are considered the standard for modern-day modeling.

nodes.label: Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC)
nodes.description: A computational algorithm used to sample from posterior distributions in Bayesian models for inference.

nodes.label: Gibbs Sampling
nodes.description: A specific MCMC algorithm that partitions the parameter vector and converts high-dimensional sampling into iteratively sampling low-dimensional conditional distributions.

nodes.label: Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC)
nodes.description: A sampling method used in Bayesian inference that is popular for its efficiency in navigating complex posterior distributions.

nodes.label: Return on Ad Spend (ROAS)
nodes.description: A key performance metric measuring the revenue generated for every dollar spent on advertising.

nodes.label: Marginal ROAS (mROAS)
nodes.description: The additional revenue generated by one-unit increase in spend, typically calculated as the derivative of predicted sales with respect to spend.

nodes.label: Optimal Media Mix
nodes.description: The allocation of media spend across channels that maximizes revenue under a budget constraint.

nodes.label: Posterior Distribution
nodes.description: The probability distribution of parameters after updating with observed data during the learning step.

nodes.label: Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC)
nodes.description: A model selection criterion that balances the need for fitting the data well against model complexity to choose appropriate specifications.

nodes.label: Control Variables
nodes.description: Non-media variables such as price, product distribution, and seasonality included in the model to account for external factors affecting sales.

nodes.label: Sales Response
nodes.description: The dependent variable in media mix models, typically representing weekly or monthly aggregated sales volume.

nodes.label: Media Spend
nodes.description: The independent variable representing the amount of money spent on advertising across different media channels.

nodes.label: STAN
nodes.description: A probabilistic programming language used to specify log density functions and perform Bayesian inference with Hamiltonian Monte Carlo sampling.

nodes.label: C++
nodes.description: A programming language used to implement custom Gibbs samplers for efficient model estimation in media mix modeling.

nodes.label: Geometric Decay
nodes.description: A functional form for the adstock weight function that assumes advertising effect peaks at the same time period as ad exposure.

nodes.label: Delayed Adstock
nodes.description: An adstock function variant that accounts for a delay in the peak effect of advertising compared to the exposure time.

nodes.label: Reach Transformation
nodes.description: A parsimonious functional form equivalent to fixing the slope parameter in the Hill function, used to model the shape effect.

nodes.label: Marketing Mix Modeling
nodes.description: A measurement methodology referenced in the document context as part of the broader measurement landscape advertisers are reevaluating.

nodes.label: Calibration
nodes.description: A process to adjust model predictions using experimental results to align with true causal effects.

nodes.label: siMMMulator
nodes.description: A data simulation package from Meta used to generate simulated MMM datasets for testing calibration.

nodes.label: GeoLift
nodes.description: A library in R provided by Meta that leverages Synthetic Control Methods for geographical testing.

nodes.label: Randomized Controlled Trials (RCT)
nodes.description: Experimental method used to generate true lift data points for model calibration purposes.

nodes.label: Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE)
nodes.description: An evaluation metric computed between the true ROAS from simulation and estimated ROAS from Robyn.

nodes.label: Meta Marketing Science
nodes.description: The organization behind Robyn, the open-source MMM package, co-authored by Gufeng Zhou and others.

nodes.label: TV Channel
nodes.description: A simulated media channel used in experiments to evaluate MMM calibration performance.

nodes.label: ROBYN HACKATHON 2022
nodes.description: The public hackathon where the Robyn package was showcased and calibration projects were evaluated.

nodes.label: Trend & Seasonality
nodes.description: Data patterns in revenue that are included in simulations to mimic real-life situations.

nodes.label: Conversions
nodes.description: The causal effect of a unit impression used directly to create RCT results for calibration.

nodes.label: Statistical Fit
nodes.description: A measure of how well a model fits the data statistically, often prioritized in traditional analytics but weighed against business logic in this context.

nodes.label: Business Logic
nodes.description: The practical reasoning and plausibility of results within a specific industry or client context, prioritized over pure statistical fit by experts.

nodes.label: Plausibility
nodes.description: The quality of being reasonable or believable, used to evaluate MMM decomposition and model outcomes against business reality.

nodes.label: MMM Decomposition
nodes.description: A key output of MMM detailing model attribution of outcomes to individual variables, typically presented as a stacked chart over time.

nodes.label: Heuristics
nodes.description: Simple rules or mental shortcuts used by practitioners to evaluate business logic and select models when specific formulas are not utilized.

nodes.label: Formulas
nodes.description: Specific mathematical expressions or heuristics utilized by some providers for evaluating model output against business logic.

nodes.label: Non-MMM measurements
nodes.description: Alternative data sources or metrics that are more accurate than MMM for certain variables, used as a benchmark for model selection.

nodes.label: Industry Norms
nodes.description: Typical values and standards specific to an industry that influence the evaluation of MMM results and model selection.

nodes.label: Client Confidence
nodes.description: The level of trust a client places in the results, considered as a factor when choosing the final model alongside statistical fit.

nodes.label: Expertise and Experience
nodes.description: The accumulated knowledge and practical judgment of practitioners used to evaluate business logic instead of specific formulas.

nodes.label: Decision-Making Processes
nodes.description: The multifaceted steps and criteria involved in selecting models, emphasizing business plausibility over statistical precision.

nodes.label: Marketing Science
nodes.description: A journal that published the survey article on decision-making processes in Marketing Mix Modelling.

nodes.id: Decision-Making Processes in Marketing Mix Modelling: A Survey of MMM Providers
nodes.label: Decision-Making Processes in Marketing Mix Modelling: A Survey of MMM Providers
Attribution Model
nodes.description: A method used to determine which marketing interactions contribute to a conversion, such as first click or last click models.

nodes.label: Incrementality tests
nodes.description: Tests evaluating the incremental impact of a channel by comparing conversion numbers between exposed and non-exposed groups.

nodes.label: Conversion Lift Tests (CLT)
nodes.description: A type of incrementality test where randomization is based on individual users, often run directly on advertiser platforms like Meta.

nodes.label: Cost per Incremental Conversion (CPIC)
nodes.description: A metric computed from incrementality tests used to adjust marketing budget allocation.

nodes.label: Meta Pixel
nodes.description: A tracking tool used in Meta Conversion Lift Tests to track conversions between control and treated groups.

nodes.id: Getting the best of both worlds in marketing incrementality tests...
nodes.label: Getting the best of both worlds in marketing incrementality tests...
nodes.description: An article within the Marketing Measurement series discussing combining Meta Conversion Lift tests with in-house geo-based tests.

nodes.label: Qonto
nodes.description: Company that published the Marketing Measurement series and operates in 4 European markets.

nodes.label: France
nodes.description: The country where the marketing experiment was conducted, divided into administrative regions.

nodes.label: Gufeng Zhou
nodes.description: Author of the Robyn MMM package and co-author of this technical note on budget allocation.

nodes.label: Bernardo Lares
nodes.description: Co-author of the technical note discussing Robyn's budget allocator scenarios.

nodes.id: Hitting ROAS target using Robyn's budget allocator
nodes.label: Hitting ROAS target using Robyn's budget allocator
nodes.description: A Medium article detailing the new 'target_efficiency' scenario in Robyn's budget allocator v3.10.1.

nodes.label: Target Efficiency
nodes.description: A new scenario in the budget allocator allowing users to set ROAS or CPA targets for optimization.

nodes.label: Max Response
nodes.description: An existing scenario in the budget allocator that maximizes response given a fixed total budget level.

nodes.label: Saturation Curve
nodes.description: A mathematical model representing the relationship between media spend and response, foundational to allocation.

nodes.label: SLSQP
nodes.description: Sequential Least Square Quadratic Programming, an optimization algorithm used by Robyn to solve allocation problems.

nodes.label: nloptr
nodes.description: A library that provides the SLSQP algorithm implementation utilized in Robyn's budget allocator.

nodes.label: Break-even
nodes.description: A business threshold where total response equals total spend, often used as a reference target (ROAS=1).

nodes.label: Diminishing Return
nodes.description: An economic concept describing how efficiency decreases as spend increases beyond a certain point.

nodes.label: BARK
nodes.description: Dog-centric company headquartered in New York offering subscription services like BarkBox and Super Chewer.

nodes.label: Michoel Snow
nodes.description: Lead Data Scientist at BARK who implemented the Robyn MMM solution.

nodes.label: New York
nodes.description: City where BARK is headquartered.

nodes.label: BarkBox
nodes.description: Monthly subscription service offered by BARK for dogs.

nodes.label: Super Chewer
nodes.description: Subscription service offered by BARK for large dog breeds.

nodes.label: BARK Bright
nodes.description: Subscription service offered by BARK for pet health and wellness.

nodes.label: BARK Eats
nodes.description: Subscription service offered by BARK for dog food.

nodes.label: Google
nodes.description: Source of seminal papers and tools like AMSS used for parameter recovery exercises in MMM.

nodes.label: Amazon
nodes.description: Online marketplace and digital advertising channel used by BARK.

nodes.label: Email
nodes.description: Digital communication channel evaluated for customer acquisition costs by BARK.

nodes.label: Linear TV
nodes.description: Traditional television advertising channel evaluated by BARK for marketing effectiveness.

nodes.label: Resident
nodes.description: An e-commerce company with digitally native home goods brands including Nectar and DreamCloud, selling products through digital channels and brick-and-mortar stores.

nodes.label: In-house Model
nodes.description: A marketing measurement solution developed by Resident's data science team before adopting Facebook's Robyn.

nodes.label: First Click
nodes.description: An attribution model type that credits the first interaction with a user for the conversion.

nodes.label: Last Click
nodes.description: An attribution model type that credits the final interaction before a conversion.

nodes.label: Revenue Growth
nodes.description: A business metric measuring the increase in sales revenue over a specific period, such as quarter-over-quarter.

nodes.label: Cost per Acquisition
nodes.description: A marketing metric representing the cost incurred to acquire one paying customer.

nodes.label: Privacy Changes
nodes.description: Upcoming shifts in the digital landscape regarding data privacy that impact marketing measurement signals and quality.

nodes.label: Offline Media
nodes.description: Traditional advertising channels such as TV, radio, or print that are distinct from digital online advertising.

nodes.label: Online Advertising
nodes.description: Digital marketing channels including social media and search ads where privacy changes significantly impact measurement.

nodes.label: Robyn 2.0
nodes.description: A newer iteration of the Robyn solution that includes support for calibration and increased granularity.

nodes.label: Avi Shenshakir
nodes.description: Vice President of Data at Resident who highlighted the importance of MMM as an additional measurement tool.

nodes.label: Incrementality
nodes.description: A metric used to validate best practices and understand if strategies are actually working through test-and-learn approaches.

nodes.label: A/B Testing
nodes.description: An experimental research method often seen as the gold standard, but deemed unsuitable here due to funnel timing issues.

nodes.label: Geographical Test
nodes.description: A testing strategy that splits a country into groups of regions to measure impact by varying budget in specific areas.

nodes.label: Synthetic Control Methods
nodes.description: A methodology popularized in the early 2000s used to construct a synthetic version of a control group based on pre-test data.

nodes.label: Online Paid Acquisition
nodes.description: One of the three categories of client acquisition, consisting mainly of clickable ads on platforms like LinkedIn and Instagram.

nodes.label: Regions
nodes.description: Administrative divisions of France used to split the market for the geographical test.

nodes.label: Sign-ups
nodes.description: A key metric tracked to measure customer acquisition and the impact of budget changes.

nodes.label: Counterfactual Group
nodes.description: A synthetic control group constructed to inform what performance would have been under business as usual configuration.

nodes.label: Experiment Protocol
nodes.description: The structured approach used to ensure causal relationships between actions and results in the study.

nodes.label: NRMSE
nodes.description: Normalized Root Mean Square Error used as an objective function to measure prediction error in Robyn.

nodes.label: DECOMP.RSSD
nodes.description: Decomposition Root Sum of Squared Distance used as an objective function to measure business error in Robyn.

nodes.label: MAPE.LIFT
nodes.description: Mean Absolute Percentage Error for experiments used as an objective function for calibration in Robyn.

nodes.label: Project Halo
nodes.description: An industry-wide collaboration providing privacy-safe cross-channel reach deduplication data for calibration.

nodes.label: K-means Clustering
nodes.description: A clustering method used by Robyn to reduce model choice among Pareto-optimal candidates.

nodes.label: Holistic Calibration
nodes.description: An advanced feature in Robyn integrating MMM, experiments, and attribution for comprehensive measurement.

nodes.label: Incrementality Test Scheduling
nodes.description: The practice of planning when and how frequently to run incrementality experiments, balancing speed of results against result quality

nodes.label: Velocity vs. Validity Trade-off
nodes.description: A fundamental tension between running many experiments quickly versus ensuring their results can be generalized beyond the specific test context

nodes.label: Internal Validity
nodes.description: The degree to which a study accurately establishes cause-effect relationships within its specific experimental design and context

nodes.label: External Validity
nodes.description: The degree to which experimental results can be generalized to other situations, populations, or settings beyond the test context

nodes.label: Marketing Experiments (XPs)
nodes.description: Incrementality tests conducted in marketing contexts to measure causal impact of marketing channels on business outcomes

nodes.label: Cookie Contamination
nodes.description: A threat to external validity where users exposed to multiple platforms' ads simultaneously cause overestimation of each platform's incremental impact

nodes.label: Multi-cell Tests
nodes.description: Advanced geo testing approach that allows running simultaneous geo tests without introducing contamination through proper randomization

nodes.label: Minimum Detectable Effect (MDE)
nodes.description: The smallest effect size an experiment can reliably detect, which influences sample size and test design requirements

nodes.label: Bayesian Marketing Mix Modeling
nodes.description: A modeling approach that incorporates experimental results as informative priors while allowing the model to disagree with them

nodes.label: Time delay
nodes.description: Also known as lag effect, this refers to the time gap between an ad exposure and the resulting increase in a target KPI like sales.

nodes.label: Time decay
nodes.description: Also known as memory or carryover effect, this refers to the gradual forgetting of an ad's impact over time after exposure.

nodes.label: Geometric Adstock
nodes.description: A specific function used in MMM to model adstock effects, known for being simple and easy to understand.

nodes.label: Weibull PDF adstock
nodes.description: A versatile adstock function capable of modeling both fixed delay and flexible decay, though trickier to optimize.

nodes.label: Linear saturation
nodes.description: A saturation curve type representing constant returns to scale, suggesting infinite increases in conversions with spend.

nodes.label: Concave saturation
nodes.description: A saturation curve type representing diminishing returns to scale, commonly used in MMM as it aligns with marketing science theory.

nodes.label: S-shaped saturation
nodes.description: A saturation curve type representing variable returns to scale, introducing the concept of a threshold before returns are seen.

nodes.label: Logistic function
nodes.description: A mathematical function used in MMM to represent concave response curves for advertising channels.

nodes.label: Dimension reduction
nodes.description: A pre-processing technique used in MMM to reduce the number of control features while retaining information and improving model parsimony.

nodes.label: Model parsimony
nodes.description: The principle of reducing the number of variables in a model to improve interpretability and avoid overfitting.

nodes.label: Streamlit demo app
nodes.description: An interactive application provided by Qonto to allow users to experiment with adstock and saturation functions in an MMM context.

nodes.label: Google MMM paper
nodes.description: A seminal research paper outlining Bayesian methods for media mix modeling with carryover and shape effects.

nodes.label: Lag effect
nodes.description: An alternative term for time delay, describing the postponement of advertising impact on KPIs.

nodes.label: Memory effect
nodes.description: An alternative term for time decay, describing how the memory of an ad fades over time.

nodes.label: Bayesian statistics
nodes.description: A statistical philosophy where probability expresses a degree of belief in an event, updated with new evidence.

nodes.label: Bayes' Theorem
nodes.description: A fundamental theorem describing how to update the probabilities of hypotheses when given evidence.

nodes.label: Prior distributions
nodes.description: Probability distributions representing beliefs about model parameters before observing data in Bayesian inference.

nodes.label: Likelihood
nodes.description: A function representing the probability of observing the data given specific parameter values in the model.

nodes.label: Marginal likelihood
nodes.description: A normalizing factor in Bayes' Theorem ensuring the final result is a valid probability.

nodes.label: Uncertainty
nodes.description: The degree of confidence in possible values of parameters, often quantified by distributions in Bayesian models.

nodes.label: PyMC
nodes.description: Tool used for specifying a Bayesian model with inputs for a Bayesian model in MMM.

nodes.label: Delphi Method
nodes.description: A technique leveraging expert opinions to define priors and mitigate bias in Bayesian MMM.

nodes.label: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
nodes.description: The cost associated with acquiring a new customer, often used to inform prior distributions for referral coefficients.

nodes.label: Streamlit app
nodes.description: A tool allowing users to interact with probability distributions and parameters defined in the article.

nodes.label: Probabilistic programming language
nodes.description: A type of programming language that allows users to specify probabilistic models for inference.

nodes.label: Customer Lifetime Value
nodes.description: A metric representing the total revenue a business can expect from a single customer account, modeled using PyMC-Marketing.

nodes.label: No-U-Turn Sampler
nodes.description: An MCMC sampling algorithm included in PyMC that explores the parameter space efficiently.

nodes.label: Metropolis sampling
nodes.description: An MCMC sampling algorithm used to explore the parameter space and focus on regions with high posterior probability.

nodes.label: Deterministic variables
nodes.description: Variables in PyMC that are dependent on other objects and fixed once the values from dependent objects are known.

nodes.label: Stochastic variables
nodes.description: Random variables in PyMC, typically represented as various types of distributions such as HalfNormal or Normal.

nodes.label: Data containers
nodes.description: A feature in PyMC that allows grouping and organizing input data into dimensions and coordinates for easier management.

nodes.label: Normal distribution
nodes.description: The likelihood function used to bridge modeled data and true observed data in the Bayesian framework.

nodes.label: Half Normal distribution
nodes.description: A probability distribution used for priors on parameters that must be non-negative, such as intercept or coefficients.

nodes.label: Beta distribution
nodes.description: A probability distribution used for adstock parameters which should be between 0 and 1.

nodes.label: Gamma distribution
nodes.description: A probability distribution used for saturation parameters like lambda which must be greater than 0.

nodes.label: Student-T distribution
nodes.description: An alternative to Normal distribution for likelihood when residuals are not evenly distributed about the mean.

nodes.label: PyMC-Marketing
nodes.description: An open-source package built on top of PyMC allowing users to build Customer Lifetime Value and Marketing Mix Models.

nodes.label: Bayesian Model
nodes.description: A type of MMM that uses Bayesian inference to estimate parameters with uncertainty and prior distributions.

nodes.label: Ruari Walker
nodes.description: The author of the article detailing how Qonto specified a Bayesian MMM using PyMC.

nodes.label: Project Robyn
nodes.description: An organization that provided the fictitious dataset used for the demonstration in the notebook.

nodes.label: MinMaxScaler
nodes.description: A scaling method that normalizes features to a fixed range, often used for exogenous variables in MMM.

nodes.label: MaxAbsScaler
nodes.description: A scaling method that scales features by their maximum absolute value, preferred for endogenous variables to allow rescaling.

nodes.label: Train Set
nodes.description: A subset of the dataset used for model training and defining the trace in Bayesian modeling.

nodes.label: Test Set
nodes.description: A subset of data held back to evaluate the validity, accuracy, and generalizability of the model.

nodes.label: Overfitting
nodes.description: Risk in MMM due to the high ratio of features-to-data that test-set validation mitigates.

nodes.label: RD-D2 shrinkage prior
nodes.description: An advanced technique using a prior on the coefficient of determination to prevent overfitting with many inputs.

nodes.label: Fourier modes
nodes.description: A mathematical tool used in the model specification to capture seasonality components in the data.

nodes.label: Linear feature
nodes.description: A type of variable transformation used to model trend components in the marketing mix model.

nodes.label: Causal DAG
nodes.description: A directed acyclic graph used to represent causal relationships between variables in the marketing mix.

nodes.label: MLOps
nodes.description: Machine Learning Operations, a set of practices integrating machine learning workflows into software development and operations lifecycle.

nodes.label: DVC
nodes.description: Data Version Control, an open-source tool for versioning data, models, pipelines, and experiments in machine learning projects.

nodes.label: MLFlow
nodes.description: An open-source platform for managing workflows and artifacts across the machine learning lifecycle, including experiment tracking and model registry.

nodes.label: Git
nodes.description: A distributed version control system used to track changes in source code and metadata for DVC projects.

nodes.label: AWS S3
nodes.description: Amazon Web Services Simple Storage Service, a cloud storage service used with DVC for remote data storage.

nodes.label: Kibana
nodes.description: A visualization tool used in the MLOps architecture for logging and monitoring MLFlow deployments.

nodes.label: Prometheus
nodes.description: An open-source systems monitoring and alerting toolkit used for monitoring MLFlow deployments.

nodes.label: Data Scientists
nodes.description: Professionals who develop and manage machine learning models, utilizing tools like DVC and MLFlow in an MLOps framework.

nodes.label: Site Reliability Engineers
nodes.description: Engineers who assist with the deployment and hosting of MLOps solutions to ensure reliability and scalability.

nodes.label: Stakeholders
nodes.description: Business or growth teams that provide prior beliefs, expert knowledge, and business logic for MMM models.

nodes.label: MLFlow Experiment Tracking
nodes.description: A component of MLFlow that allows logging and tracking of experiments, metrics, parameters, and artifacts.

nodes.label: MLFlow Model Registry
nodes.description: A component of MLFlow providing a central repository for managing and versioning models.

nodes.label: Marketing Measurement Stack
nodes.description: Broader framework where MMM works as a component, complementing attribution models and incrementality experiments.

nodes.label: Linear Regression
nodes.description: Simple method possible for MMM but not recommended compared to Bayesian approaches.

nodes.label: Aggregated Data
nodes.description: Type of data used by MMMs that requires no Personally Identifiable Information (PII) to be run.

nodes.label: Offline Marketing Channels
nodes.description: Channels such as TV, Out-of-Home, or Radio whose efficiencies are typically hard to measure but can be analyzed by MMMs.

nodes.label: Out-of-sample Validation
nodes.description: Method used to evaluate how the MMM will generalize to new data and mitigate overfitting risk.

nodes.label: Root-Mean-Square-Error (RMSE)
nodes.description: Genuine error metric for model evaluation that should be viewed alongside R-Squared values.

nodes.label: R-Squared
nodes.description: Metric indicating the proportion of variance of a KPI explained by the MMM, which can be easily increased by adding variables.

nodes.label: Confidence Intervals
nodes.description: Indispensable insight into the uncertainty of model results that should be demanded from consultancies.

nodes.label: Open Source
nodes.description: Rapidly developing field for MMM solutions where Qonto has experimented with several options.

nodes.label: Consultancy Route
nodes.description: Option for building and delivering MMMs via external vendors or consultancies.

nodes.label: In-house Route
nodes.description: Option for building MMMs internally within a company's data team.

nodes.label: Measurement 360
nodes.description: A measurement framework developed by Meta and Deloitte that adopts an agile measurement strategy to deliver a holistic view of campaign performance.

nodes.label: Deloitte
nodes.description: The consulting firm that collaborated with Meta to develop the Measurement 360 framework.

nodes.label: First-party data
nodes.description: Data type emphasized in the framework's strategy to enable privacy by design and rely on available data sources.

nodes.label: Data privacy
nodes.description: A key value placed by consumers that influences the need for future-proof methodologies and privacy-safe measurement strategies.

nodes.label: Campaign performance
nodes.description: The primary metric outcome that Measurement 360 aims to deliver a holistic view of across multiple channels.

nodes.label: Customer journey
nodes.description: The customer lifecycle path that the Measurement 360 framework helps marketers gain a holistic view of in an agile way.

nodes.label: Test-and-learn approach
nodes.description: An operationalized methodology within the framework to validate best practices and gain insight into incrementality.

nodes.label: Robyn v3.8
nodes.description: An open-source MMM package from Meta that introduced a new contextual calibration feature to isolate immediate and carryover effects.

nodes.label: Correlation
nodes.description: A statistical relationship between variables that does not imply causation, which is the core nature of traditional MMM.

nodes.label: Causation
nodes.description: The cause-and-effect relationship between variables, which experiments aim to measure rather than mere correlation.

nodes.label: Immediate Effect
nodes.description: The short-term impact of advertising on consumer behavior that occurs during the study period in an experiment.

nodes.label: Naïve Calibration
nodes.description: An approach that simply drives MMM's total predicted effect closer to causal estimates, often leading to underestimation of calibrated channels.

nodes.label: Contextual Calibration
nodes.description: A feature in Robyn v3.8 that isolates immediate and carryover effects to calibrate only the appropriate subset of MMM's total effect.

nodes.label: Last Dollar Impact
nodes.description: The incremental impact measured by experiments where the absence of a tested channel is filled by others, representing the lower bound of effect.

nodes.label: Average Dollar Impact
nodes.description: The long-term average impact of ads on all outcomes measured by MMM within the modeling window.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: MMM relies on regression modeling to derive an equation that describes the KPI and quantify incremental impact.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Ridge regression is a common regularization technique used within MMM to prevent overfitting and address multicollinearity.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Model selection in Robyn involves configuring Ridge regression parameters like lambda to control penalty and variance.

links.label: SUPPORTS
links.description: Robyn implements Ridge regression to counter multicollinearity issues common in marketing mix models.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Adstock is a component concept included in the specification of an MMM model.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Time delay is one of the two important marketing effects encapsulated by the Adstock concept.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Time decay is one of the two important marketing effects encapsulated by the Adstock concept.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Geometric Adstock is a specific function type available for modeling adstock effects in an MMM regression equation.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Weibull PDF adstock is a specific function type available for modeling adstock effects, capable of modeling both fixed delay and flexible decay.

links.label: REFERENCES
links.description: The Streamlit demo app is used to experiment with and visualize Adstock functions and parameters.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Beta distribution is used for the prior on adstock parameters which should be between 0 and 1.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Adstock transformations are a component of the model specification block in the Bayesian MMM.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Adstocking transformation is applied on input media variables to account for the immediate and carryover portion of transformed variables.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Saturation is a component concept included in the specification of an MMM model.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: The Hill function is the specific mathematical implementation used by Robyn to model saturation curves.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Linear saturation is one of the four types of saturation curves representing constant returns to scale.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Concave saturation is one of the four types of saturation curves representing diminishing returns to scale.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: S-shaped saturation is one of the four types of saturation curves representing variable returns to scale with a threshold.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Gamma distribution is advised for the prior on saturation parameters like lambda which must be greater than 0.

links.label: PART_OF

links.description: MLFlow is a versatile platform used within an MLOps framework for managing workflows, experiment tracking, and model registry.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Kibana is utilized within the MLOps architecture for logging and visualization of MLFlow deployments.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Prometheus is utilized within the MLOps architecture for monitoring the performance and health of ML systems.

links.label: SUPPORTS
links.description: Data scientists are key users of MLOps tools like DVC and MLFlow to manage their modeling workflows.

links.label: SUPPORTS
links.description: SREs assist in deploying MLOps tools as hosted solutions and ensuring the infrastructure supports data science needs.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Experiment tracking is one of the two most important components of MLFlow within an MLOps workflow.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: The Model Registry is a central repository component of MLFlow used for managing and versioning models in MLOps.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Git tracks metadata for DVC projects while DVC handles versioning of data and artifacts themselves.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: AWS S3 is used as a remote storage backend for DVC to manage large datasets in multi-person projects.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: R-Squared should be viewed alongside genuine error metrics such as Root-Mean-Square-Error for evaluation.

links.label: SUPPORTS
links.description: Demanding confidence intervals provides indispensable insight into uncertainty alongside error metrics like RMSE.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: These are two alternative options for producing MMMs, one via external vendors and one internally.

links.label: CREATED_BY
links.description: The Measurement 360 framework was developed by Deloitte in collaboration with Meta.

links.label: SUPPORTS
links.description: The framework is designed to deliver a holistic view of campaign performance across a wider range of channels.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: The framework encourages building more advanced analytics through first-party data as part of its future-proof strategy.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: The framework aligns with the increasing consumer value placed on data privacy and security in the advertising landscape.

links.label: RELATED_TO
links.description: Through Measurement 360, marketers can gain a holistic view of the customer journey in an agile, privacy-safe way.

links.label: SUPPORTS
links.description: Relying on first-party data wherever possible enables privacy by design in the measurement strategy.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: Contextual calibration is a specific feature launched in the Robyn v3.8 release to tackle differences in scope.

links.label: OPPOSES
links.description: Correlation does not equal causation, which is a fundamental distinction between MMM and experimental methods.

links.label: PART_OF
links.description: The new robyn_calibrate function in Robyn v3.8 separates the immediate effect from carryover effect for calibration.

links.label: OPPOSES
links.description: Contextual calibration in Robyn v3.8 offers an improvement over common naïve calibration methods that underestimate media.
